project_overview.project_development_objective: To support the development of a more adaptive social protection system that will contribute to any future crisis-resilience among poor and vulnerable households
project_overview.beneficiaries: Poor and vulnerable households across Pakistan; expansion from 4.6 million to 7 million BISP beneficiary families; informal sector workers; out-of-school children (particularly girls); pregnant women and mothers; vulnerable populations affected by climate shocks

primary_locations: All provinces and regions of Pakistan
13 districts for hybrid SP scheme pilot
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - Federally Administered Areas and Kalash valleys included; program designed to address vulnerabilities in conflict-affected and disaster-prone regions; climate shocks and natural disasters are primary fragility factors

key_components.component_name: Results Area 1: Building Crisis-Resilient Delivery Systems
key_components.description: Support for innovations to social registry (NSER) including multi-entry localized access (MELA) mechanism for regular household data updates; systematization of cross-checks and big data analytics for improved targeting; beneficiary-centric biometric payment systems; provincial governance structure for NSER; data exchange interfaces with other government programs

key_components.component_name: Results Area 2: Developing Innovative Hybrid Social Protection Scheme
key_components.description: Hybrid scheme blending social assistance with social risk mitigation for informal sector workers; contributory savings scheme with government matching incentives; support for women and exiting BISP beneficiaries in socioeconomic bands I and II; digital platform for tracking contributions and withdrawals; partnership with financial institutions

key_components.component_name: Results Area 3: Protecting Human Capital in Early and Primary School Years
key_components.description: Nutrition-focused conditional cash transfer (Nashonuma) linked to maternal and child health services; expansion of Waseela-e-Taleem (WeT) education-linked CCT; completion incentives for girls transitioning to secondary school; federal-provincial coordination mechanisms; impact evaluations

Support for design and evaluation of MELA mechanism; data sharing protocol review for personal data protection; assessment of big data effectiveness for targeting; social registry governance support; payment system innovations; CCT design and implementation; capacity building for BISP staff; M&E strengthening

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Benazir Income Support Programme (BISP), headed by a Chairperson with Management Board including governmental and non-governmental members; Secretary BISP heads Management with Director Generals in headquarters and four provinces/two regions
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Five operational wings: NSER Wing (RA1), Complementary Initiatives Wing (RA2), CCT Wing (RA3), Cash Transfer Wing (cross-support), Technology Wing (all RAs); leverages intergovernmental coordination structures under National Social Protection Program (NSPP); federal-provincial coordination for human capital interventions; partnership with NADRA for identity verification; coordination with provincial education and health departments
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: No procurement activities required under PforR in accordance with World Bank Policy; TA component procurement follows World Bank Procurement Regulations; existing IVA (Planning Commission of Pakistan) to be used for verification of results
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: BISP responsible for M&E using existing systems; bi-annual reviews analyzing results and progress; financial audit; Management Information System (MIS) tracking; continuous process evaluation and spot-checks; beneficiary satisfaction surveys; quarterly program progress reports; baseline and impact evaluation analysis; Mid-Term Review Report; disaggregation by gender and province; Independent Verification Agency (IVA) verification of Disbursement Linked Results (DLRs)

identified_risks.risk_description: Need for intergovernmental collaboration and limited provincial ownership of federal programs; political instability affecting program continuity
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Leverage intergovernmental structures developed under NSPP; BISP has demonstrated track record of managing such risks and reaching consensus between federal and provincial authorities; establishment of provincially representative governance structure for NSER; federal-provincial coordination mechanisms

identified_risks.risk_description: COVID-19 pandemic causing economic contraction (GDP declined from 1.9% in FY19 to -1.5% in FY20); tight fiscal situation; vulnerability to external shocks; subdued near-term economic prospects with growth projected at 1.3% for FY2021-22
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Government has given highest priority to mitigating socio-economic impacts of pandemic; reprioritized resources from existing budget for social protection; previous performance and medium-term budgetary framework suggest projected 3% annual increase in BISP budget will occur; Government committed to spending US$6.5 billion over five years under Ehsaas; access to Debt Service Suspension Initiative (DSSI) providing US$1.6-2.4 billion in temporary fiscal space

identified_risks.risk_category: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability
identified_risks.risk_description: Need for capacity building for new initiatives under RA1 (MELA mechanism, big data analytics); complexity of nutrition-focused CCT requiring coordination across multiple agencies; staff turnover affecting contract management; delays in awarding contracts
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: TA component provides support for capacity building; BISP has demonstrated capacity managing NSPP; existing IVA (Planning Commission) to be used reducing verification delays; institutionalization of processes to address staff turnover; delegation of financial powers for more efficient contract payment; training on E-waste management; development of Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)

identified_risks.risk_category: Technical Design of Project or Program
identified_risks.risk_description: Complexity of implementing new systems (MELA, hybrid SP scheme, nutrition CCT); targeting accuracy challenges; data quality and protection concerns; integration of multiple data sources and systems
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Program is technically sound and consistent with international experience; BISP is efficient with cost-to-transfer ratio declining from 3% to 1.7%; TA component supports design and evaluation; review of data sharing protocols for personal data protection; pilot testing of survey approaches for climate-related disaster targeting; impact evaluations planned for CCT programs

identified_risks.risk_description: Financial management and procurement risks; need for improved monitoring to prevent, detect, and rectify improprieties; delays in external audit follow-up; limited public reporting of audit results; potential for fraud or errors in payments
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: FSA rates current risk as Moderate with residual risk for FM and procurement rated Low; BISP has satisfactory performance under NSPP; adoption of International Public Sector Accounting Standards; development of reporting templates; improved inhouse capacity for information system audits; interface with banks to detect improper payments; online complaint management system for procurements; commitment to ACG compliance; annual reporting to World Bank on ACG compliance

identified_risks.risk_description: Low environmental risks from ICT e-waste; moderate social risks related to targeting accuracy, inclusion/exclusion errors in new programs, vulnerability of marginalized groups, gender gaps, data privacy concerns, potential for adverse effects on vulnerable populations
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: ESSA concluded program activities likely to provide significant social benefits; development and implementation of SOPs for e-waste management; training on e-waste management for BISP, NADRA, and EPA staff; Stakeholder Engagement Plan (SEP) with mechanisms for Indigenous and marginalized communities feedback; differentiated engagement measures; grievance redress mechanism in place; review of data sharing protocols for personal data protection; gender-disaggregated monitoring; focus on inclusion of vulnerable groups

identified_risks.risk_description: Potential security challenges in conflict-affected areas and Federally Administered Areas; disruption of service delivery from climate-related shocks; vulnerability of payment systems and access points to disruption
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Program designed to build resilience to shocks; beneficiary-centric payment system designed for resilience against service disruptions; MELA access points to be made resilient against climate shocks; integration of information from National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) and Pakistan Met Department on climatic hazards; adaptive SP systems to reduce lead time for crisis response

identified_risks.risk_description: Need for stakeholder buy-in for new programs; potential resistance to changes in delivery systems; coordination challenges across multiple agencies and provinces
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Stakeholder Engagement Plan developed; communication strategy for NSER through BISP field offices, local public institutions, print/electronic media; BISP has demonstrated ability to reach consensus; leveraging existing coordination structures; beneficiary satisfaction surveys planned

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS2: Stakeholder Engagement and Information Disclosure
ESS10: Indigenous Peoples/Sub-Saharan African Historically Underserved Traditional Local Communities
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: Yes - Existing program GRM in place; World Bank's Grievance Redress Service (GRS) available for complaints; World Bank Inspection Panel available for complaints regarding Bank non-compliance; GRM improvement and implementation planned as part of Program Action Plan; semi-annual reporting on GRM implementation required
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Over 53% of out-of-school children are girls; gender parity index at primary level is 0.832 and at lower secondary is 0.837; program addresses gender gaps through: expansion of Waseela-e-Taleem (WeT) with focus on girls' enrollment and attendance; DLI 6 measures girls receiving completion incentives for transition to secondary school; DLI 11 sets gender-disaggregated targets for children completing primary education; only 7% of women have bank/mobile money accounts vs 34.6% of men - program expands financial inclusion for women through hybrid SP scheme and One Woman One Account Policy; maternal mortality rate of 140 per 100,000 live births addressed through nutrition-focused CCT linked to health services; gender-disaggregated monitoring of all indicators
vulnerable_groups: Informal sector workers (72% of non-agricultural workers, 81.6% of private sector employment)
The 'missing middle' - those without access to insurance or savings mechanisms
Out-of-school children (11.5 million at primary level)
Populations affected by displacement or disasters

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Program designed to build crisis resilience and adaptive social protection systems responsive to shocks including conflict-related disruptions; implementation in Federally Administered Areas and conflict-affected regions; integration of disaster management information (NDMA) into targeting systems; beneficiary-centric payment systems designed for resilience against service disruptions; MELA access points to be made resilient; program supports rapid vertical and horizontal expansion of safety nets to respond to crises as demonstrated during COVID-19; focus on preventing negative coping strategies (reducing human capital investments, asset depletion) that can be exacerbated by conflict
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Pakistan faces multiple fragility factors: (1) Climate vulnerability - highly vulnerable to climatic shocks with extreme events disrupting human and physical capital; floods affected 30+ million since 2010 with damages exceeding US$14 billion; vulnerable to droughts, heatwaves, cyclones; (2) Economic fragility - real GDP growth declined from 1.9% to -1.5% in FY20 (first contraction in decades); subdued near-term prospects; tight fiscal constraints; (3) Human capital challenges - high stunting rates (37.6% of children under five); large gender gaps in schooling; 11.5 million out-of-school children; low maternal health indicators; (4) Informal economy dominance - 72% of non-agricultural workers in informal sector with limited social protection coverage; (5) Pandemic impacts - COVID-19 expected to lead to sizeable increase in poverty, reversing 20 years of progress; increased out-of-school children; deterioration of health indicators; (6) Institutional challenges - need for federal-provincial coordination; limited provincial ownership of federal programs; (7) Vulnerability of missing middle - millions without access to insurance or savings mechanisms
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Program includes implementation in Federally Administered Areas and Kalash valleys with potential security challenges; design incorporates resilience to service disruptions from security incidents; beneficiary-centric payment system designed for resilience; MELA access points to be made resilient; program supports rapid crisis response capacity as demonstrated during COVID-19; focus on preventing negative coping strategies that can be exacerbated by insecurity; integration of disaster management information systems; adaptive SP systems to reduce lead time for response to shocks
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: No specific mention of refugee populations in the PAD; however, program is designed to address vulnerabilities of displaced populations and those affected by disasters; Federally Administered Areas (historically hosting displaced populations) are included in program coverage; program's adaptive SP systems and rapid response mechanisms are relevant for crisis-displaced populations; nutrition-focused CCT and education support (WeT) relevant for vulnerable children including those displaced by conflicts or disasters
